text.p: POSTGRADUATE STUDIES
26 February, 2009
Dear Mr
ADMISSION AND REGISTRATION FOR HIGHER DEGREE COURSE

With reference to your application for admission to a higher degree of this University in the Department of Archaeology and Anthropology, it is my pleasure to inform you that the application is successful. Your registration will be for the degree of M.Phil/Ph.D [Anthropology] in the first instance, while upgrading for candidature for the Ph.D will depend on your performance at the end of the first calendar year examination.
1. Please take note of the following conditions relating to your registration.
(a) Higher degree students are expected to commence their courses at the beginning of Session for the 2008/09 Session.

(b) You will be required to present the originals of your educational/other relevant credentials, including the NYSC Discharge/Exemption Certificate, and obtain a clearance from the appropriate officer of the Postgraduate School before you proceed to make payment of the prescribed fees.
(c) You will thereafter be required to make appropriate payment in accordance with the Financial Regulation and obtain the Bursary's financial clearance on your Registration Forms.
(d) This offer is subject to your producing the documents in (b & c) above and also fulfilling all financial requirements.
2. Information on the Financial Regulations of the University for Higher Degree/Diploma can be found on the PG School Web site .

3. If you are a foreign student, a copy of our letter to the Director-General, Federal Ministry of Internal Affairs, Abuja is enclosed herewith for your necessary action. When replying for entry permit or visa, you are requested to furnish him with the following items of information to accelerate action on your application.
(a) Your nationality (b) Date and place of birth (c) Passport number, date and place of issue (d) Validity of passport (e) Sponsorship (f) Anticipated length of stay at the University of Ibadan.
It is important to note that a residence permit is necessary for the confirmation of an offer of admission.

4. Very limited Hall accommodation is available for full-time Higher Degree students and will be allocated on the basis of first-come first-served. Students are advised to get in touch with the Lodgings Officer, Student Affairs Division, University of Ibadan. You can find additional information on lodging on the PG School Web site.
5. You are required to register for your Programme of study within five weeks of the beginning of the academic year and renew your registration annually until you finally complete your programme.
6. If you accept the offer, kindly print and complete 2 copies of the Acceptance Form on the Web site and return same to me immediately.

7. Please note that the offer may be revoked and your place given to someone else, if within three weeks from the date of this letter you have not competed and returned the printed and completed acceptance forms.

8. It is mandatory that you are present for your clearance at the Admissions Office of the Postgraduate School, between Thursday, 26 February 2009 and Friday, 27 March 2009. Please note that this offer stands forfeited, if you are not cleared within those dates.
cc: Dean, Faculty of Arts
Head, Department of Archaeology and Anthropology
Yours sincerely,
Secretary, Postgraduate School
NB: This Offer of Admission is also subject to the receipt of your Masters Certificate and First Degree Certificate.
